weather.summary: Saturday: 10°C, light rain. Sunday: 10°C, overcast clouds

metadata.greeting: This weekend in Hackney, we've got a mix of indoor and outdoor activities to keep your little one entertained despite the weather! From free museum visits with hands-on exhibits to exciting Easter bonnet parades and animal encounters, there's something for everyone.

activities.title: Young V&A
activities.description: A museum designed specifically for children with hands-on activities, workshops, and exhibitions to encourage imagination, play, and design.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers sensory play areas suitable for toddlers and interactive exhibits to capture their attention.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station.
transport.tip: Check for special events and workshops on their website.

activities.title: Biodiversity Playground
activities.description: A beautifully designed wooden playground with a sweet pond theme, perfect for toddlers.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a safe and engaging space for toddlers to explore and play outdoors.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford station.
transport.tip: Combine with a visit to the Westfield Stratford City shopping centre for lunch.

activities.title: Discover Children's Story Centre
activities.description: Immersive play spaces and a story garden playground for young children.
activities.whyItWorks: Features interactive storytelling sessions, crafts, and sing-alongs suitable for babies and toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Stratford station.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for baby storytelling and singalong times.

activities.title: Museum of the Home
activities.description: Explore the history of homes and domestic life through a series of room displays.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a sensory experience for toddlers with different textures and objects to explore, plus gardens to wander in.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Hoxton station.
transport.tip: Check out the gardens for a relaxing break.

activities.title: Hackney City Farm
activities.description: A community farm with a variety of animals, gardens, and activities for children.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides a hands-on experience with animals and nature, perfect for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Located in Hackney; walking distance for many.
transport.tip: Check for special events and feeding times.

activities.title: Spitalfields Bonnet Parade
activities.description: Show up in your most creative headwear for an East London spin on a spring tradition.  Judging and prize giving follows at 2pm.
activities.whyItWorks: A fun, free and engaging parade where locals show off their Easter bonnets.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Shoreditch High Street Station.
transport.tip: Get there early to secure a good viewing spot.

activities.title: Vauxhall City Farm Easter Activities
activities.description: Free Easter hunts and bonnet-making crafts.
activities.whyItWorks: Animal-lovers will enjoy meeting the farm's goats, sheep, and smaller animals.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Victoria Line to Vauxhall Station.
transport.tip: Check for specific activity times.

activities.title: Museum of London Docklands
activities.description: Interactive exhibits on the history of London's waterfront, with a focus on children.
activities.whyItWorks: Features hands-on activities, child-friendly explanations, and a soft play area.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Canary Wharf station.
transport.tip: Look for the red signs explaining things in a child-friendly way.

activities.title: Natural History Museum
activities.description: Explore dinosaurs, animals, and natural wonders in this iconic museum.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers interactive exhibits and a dedicated children's area to engage young minds.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington station.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Science Museum
activities.description: Discover how the world works with hands-on exhibits and interactive displays.
activities.whyItWorks: The Garden in the basement is an interactive space for younger children to explore construction, water, light, and sound through play.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington station.
transport.tip: Book tickets in advance to avoid queues.

activities.title: Golders Hill Park Zoo
activities.description: A small, free zoo in Hampstead Heath with rare species of birds and animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Features wallabies, lemurs, and Scottish wildcats, offering a fun and educational experience for toddlers.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Hampstead Heath station, then a bus.
transport.tip: Combine with a picnic in Hampstead Heath.

activities.title: Horniman Museum and Gardens
activities.description: Explore natural history, musical instruments, and anthropology exhibits, plus gardens and a small animal farm.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers free workshops for children, beautiful gardens with views over London, farm animals, and a music laboratory.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Forest Hill station.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for family events and activities.

activities.title: Battersea Park
activities.description: A scenic escape with lush gardens, a boating lake, playgrounds, and a mini zoo.
activities.whyItWorks: Features a pagoda, lakeside paths, and child-friendly activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Clapham Junction, then a bus.
transport.tip: Pack a picnic to enjoy by the lake.

activities.title: Richmond Park
activities.description: Spot wild deer roaming freely in this expansive Royal Park.
activities.whyItWorks: An exciting experience for toddlers to connect with nature and wildlife.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Richmond station.
transport.tip: Bring binoculars for better deer spotting.

activities.title: Diana Memorial Playground
activities.description: A popular park with an enormous Peter Pan-inspired pirate ship and surrounding beach, sensory trail, and teepees.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers a variety of play structures and activities to keep toddlers engaged.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Notting Hill Gate station.
transport.tip: Arrive early as it can get crowded.

activities.title: Hampton Court Palace Gardens
activities.description: Roam through lush gardens, keeping your eyes peeled for elusive Lindt GOLD BUNNY statues peeking out from colourful daffodils.
activities.whyItWorks: A chocolate treat awaits at the end!

transport.fromHackney: Take the train from Waterloo to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Book in advance.

activities.title: Octonauts Above and Beyond at Kew Gardens
activities.description: Join the Octonauts on an exciting outdoor mission at Kew Gardens. An interactive trail that invites young explorers to complete challenges, discover facts, and learn how to protect our planet.
activities.whyItWorks: Combines conservation messaging with interactive play, perfect for burning off energy.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Kew Gardens station.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.

activities.title: Easter Family Activities
activities.description: Discover how Victorian engineers and inventors shaped the modern world through making, movement and mechanical marvels.
activities.whyItWorks: Drop-in throughout the day.

transport.fromHackney: Take the DLR to Tower Gateway station.
transport.tip: Book in advance to avoid disappointment.

activities.title: Family Fun
activities.address: Cambridge Heath Rd, Bethnal Green, London E2 9PA
activities.description: Get crafty at the origami workshop, learn how to design collages and more.
activities.whyItWorks: Offers fun, hands-on experiences.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Central Line to Bethnal Green station.
transport.tip: Check the schedule for family events and activities.

activities.title: Easter Family Festival
activities.description: Free family-friendly activities.
activities.whyItWorks: Provides learning, discovery and hands-on fun.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Piccadilly Line to South Kensington station.
transport.tip: Visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds.

activities.title: Easter Events
activities.description: Easter-themed egg hunts and seasonal workshops. Explore historic rooms, gardens, and the palace grounds while participating in fun activities for children.
activities.whyItWorks: The combination of royal history, spring flowers, and family-friendly events makes this an ideal destination.

transport.fromHackney: Take the train from Waterloo to Hampton Court.
transport.tip: Check the website for Easter events.

activities.title: Easter Eggsplorers
activities.description: Line-up of activities including Hunt the Giant Easter Egg Trail, Meet the Easter Bunny, Indoor Playbarn, Marshmallow Toasting, Bubble Play and Zoo and Farm Animals.
activities.whyItWorks: Lots of choice with indoor and outdoor activities.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Piccadilly Line to Hounslow West.
transport.tip: Allow 4-5 hours to explore everything.

activities.title: Spring Family Fun
activities.price: Some paid activities and some free
activities.description: A festival of fun and creativity for families at the Southbank Centre includes super shows, musical adventures, amazing puppetry and lots of free fun in the Easter break. Also worth visiting is the REPLAY: A Limitless Recycled Playground – a play space made out of repurposed materials (£7.50).
activities.whyItWorks: Many free activities and central location.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Jubilee Line to Waterloo Station.
transport.tip: Check the website for the full schedule.

activities.title: Easter at London Zoo
activities.description: London Zoo's Zoonormous Easter Egg Hunt. Kids are invited to explore London Zoo on a special Easter egg hunt, searching for six giant Easter eggs hidden around the park.
activities.whyItWorks: Explore a variety of incredible wildlife.

transport.fromHackney: Take the Overground to Camden Town, then a bus.
transport.tip: Book tickets online in advance.
